finding: Canonical names are minute-precision, so two legacy files differing only in the seconds field canonicalize to the same target. Migration renames the first and safely skips the second (no data loss) but only prints a terse 'SKIP (target exists)', leaving a confusing half-migrated pair.
proposed_action: Explanatory collision message in migration (keep safe skip) + doc minute-precision.

finding: filter sends an empty or whitespace-only input document to the LLM (real API call/cost) instead of refusing early.
evidence: repro: empty .restart.md wrote output after empty send
proposed_action: Refuse empty/whitespace input in cli_filter before the API call.

finding: Whitespace-only --scope ('   ') passes the 'at least one of --project/--scope' check (if scope:), yielding a meaningless scope and a ...session.compacted.md name. Empty-string and None correctly error.
proposed_action: Strip --scope and treat all-whitespace as absent before the check.

finding: canonical_recovery_name accepts a bogus kind with no validation (kind='bogus' -> ...x.bogus.md), which parse_recovery_name then cannot classify.
proposed_action: Validate kind in RECOVERY_KINDS (raise ValueError on bad kind).

finding: parse_recovery_name suffix match is case-sensitive; *.RESTART.MD is unrecognized so migration silently ignores it. Harmless on Linux; matters on case-insensitive FS (macOS).
proposed_action: Case-insensitive suffix match; preserve original-case session id.

finding: Legacy date-only name whose session id begins with 8 digits (12345678-ses.restart.md) mis-splits the leading digits as a failed date. Theoretical for real ses_ ids; canonical form round-trips correctly.
proposed_action: Document the date-only legacy ambiguity in a comment; add a behavior-pinning test. Not worth code (unreachable for real ids).

finding: Invalid embedded dates (month 99, hour 25) correctly yield dt=None so downstream falls back to mtime - the intended safe behavior.
proposed_action: Add regression tests pinning dt=None-on-invalid-date; no code change.
